plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-317 RP09.P07.M07.S05-RP09.P07.M09.S02 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-317 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP09.P07.M07 test/golden failure tail (missing resource through human escalation note, 18 rows), the full M08 Hermes Evidence Packet failure cycle (20 rows), and the M09 Claude Review Packet head (failure taxonomy, missing tenant failure) across three sections over 40 planned rows with a pack-local row-extras constant, Hermes evidence (H09), Claude review packet (C09), and CP00-318 handoff, without opening rollback/retry runtime, lock acquisition, test runtime paths, integration smoke runtime, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-317 is a descriptor-only CRM-core failure/Hermes slice pack (RP09.P07.M07.S05–RP09.P07.M09.S02, risk B, 40 units) and its pack-level claims are supported. The CP317 pack binding, contract current_pack, closeout-pack-plan entry, and review baseline are mutually consistent (40 units; deliverable distribution failure_recovery 22 / security_audit 4 / implementation 3 / hermes_evidence 4 / fixture 2 / test 4 / claude_review 1; micro-title counts 18/20/2). createCrmCoreCp317FailureHermesSliceDescriptor/CaseSet are descriptor-only: freezeCp317Result forces all runtime/write/object-storage/real-data/Hermes-receipt flags to false, and validateCrmCoreCp317FailureHermesSliceDescriptor enforces descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only, raw_payload_included=false on every row plus per-row guards (cross-tenant deny, no permission/audit-hint/blocked-claim leak, no lock acquisition, no retry/rollback runtime, no real fixture data, no test/integration-smoke runtime, no Hermes runtime receipt, no Claude final approval, human escalation required). Those guard fields are genuinely present via the inherited CP312/CP313 ROW_EXTRAS spread chain, so validation is non-vacuous; a scan of the changed source and contract found no runtime/real-data flag set to true. production_ready is correctly false (status implemented_pending_review); the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification marker, not an enterprise/production claim. Authority boundary is intact: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, and the no-write attestation sets promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false. Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor-only review packet/receipt pattern (no product runtime). Tests (model.test.js CP317 cases) and the contract validate script (deepEqual on binding/requirements/attestation, Claude-not-final-approver assertions) cover the boundaries, and command-evidence records all build/test/validate commands at exit 0. For CP178+ hardening: this is the pending review run itself (manifest pack_level_claude_review.status=pending, no prior valid/invalid attempts); the readiness auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON; downstream artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly not yet present. index.js is unchanged because it re-exports via wildcard. One non-blocking P3: the review harness's changed-file-scope.json double-classifies an unrelated onboarding doc. No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack closeout is not blocked.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies an unrelated onboarding doc as an implementation file
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is listed under untracked_implementation_files (line 103) while also being listed under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 109). Inspection of the file shows it is a generic new-session onboarding snapshot refreshed 2026-06-08 that still references CP00-082/CP00-083 as the live cursor, so it is unrelated to CP00-317 and is not referenced by the CP00-317 manifest implementation_refs, evidence_refs, or required_pack_artifacts. The classification is therefore self-contradictory, but the file is not load-bearing for this pack and the active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted list is empty.
findings.recommendation: Treat new-session-handoff.md strictly as an unrelated preserved dirty file for CP00-317 (the prompt directive 'do not infer unrelated dirty files are part of this pack' applies). Optionally correct the scope-file generator so a file is not emitted in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. No action required for CP00-317 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_informational; does not block pack or goal closeout; scope-file classification cleanup is optional and belongs to the review-harness generator, not the CP00-317 deliverable

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-317 crm core failure hermes slice descriptor pack
